document_metadata.title: Agentic Memory: Learning Unified Long-Term and Short-Term Memory Management for Large Language Model Agents

abstract.problem: Large language model (LLM) agents face fundamental limitations in long-horizon reasoning due to finite context windows, making effective memory management critical.
abstract.existing_limitations: Existing methods typically handle long-term memory (LTM) and short-term memory (STM) as separate components, relying on heuristics or auxiliary controllers, which limits adaptability and end-to-end optimization.
abstract.proposed_solution: Agentic Memory (AgeMem) - a unified framework that integrates LTM and STM management directly into the agent's policy
abstract.key_innovation: Memory operations exposed as tool-based actions, enabling the LLM agent to autonomously decide what and when to store, retrieve, update, summarize, or discard information
abstract.training_approach: Three-stage progressive reinforcement learning strategy with step-wise GRPO to address sparse and discontinuous rewards
abstract.results: Consistently outperforms strong memory-augmented baselines across multiple LLM backbones, achieving improved task performance, higher-quality long-term memory, and more efficient context usage

C1.name: Functional heterogeneity coordination
C1.description: LTM and STM serve distinct yet complementary purposes - designing a unified mechanism that orchestrates their interplay synergistically

C2.name: Training paradigm mismatch
C2.description: Standard RL assumes continuous trajectories with stable rewards, which conflicts with the fragmented and discontinuous experiences produced by memory operations

C3.name: Practical deployment constraints
C3.description: Integrating unified memory management directly into an agent without dependence on external expert models

components: Task specification: T (query q, contextual info I_q, expected answer A_q)

problem_formulation.action_space: Hybrid actions including language generation and memory operations

STM_tools.name: RETRIEVE
STM_tools.function: Retrieve entries from M_t to C_t

STM_tools.name: FILTER
STM_tools.function: Filter out irrelevant segments from C_t

stages.name: LTM Construction
stages.description: Agent engages in casual interactions and stores useful information into LTM

stages.name: STM Control under Distractors
stages.description: Introduces distracting/irrelevant content, requiring STM management through selective retention and compression
stages.context_behavior: C_t is reset to prevent information leakage

stages.name: Integrated Reasoning and Memory Coordination
stages.description: Presents task requiring coordinated use of both retained context and accumulated LTM
stages.context_behavior: Must retrieve from M_t and manage C_t to generate final answer

three_stage_progressive_rl.key_design: LTM M_t persists across all stages; Context C_t reset between Stage 1 and 2

step_wise_grpo.broadcasting: Terminal advantage A_T is broadcast to all preceding steps: A_t^(k,q) = A_T^(k,q)
step_wise_grpo.purpose: Enables long-range credit assignment across heterogeneous stages

task_completion.description: Assesses whether agent solves task correctly using LLM-based judge

components: Preventive actions: early summarization/filtering to avoid overflow
Information preservation: penalizes loss of critical query-related content
Storage quality: fraction of high-quality, reusable entries
Semantic relevance: LLM-based score between retrieved memories and query

component_contribution.note: Progressive improvements confirm unified memory management with end-to-end RL is essential

configurations.description: AgeMem-RAG with RL and LTM tools

reward_function_ablation.finding: Full reward function leads to significantly faster convergence and higher final performance with better memory quality

key_contributions: Proposed Agentic Memory (AgeMem) - unified framework for autonomous LTM and STM management
Developed three-stage progressive RL strategy with step-wise GRPO mechanism for effective end-to-end learning
Conducted comprehensive evaluations demonstrating robustness and effectiveness across multiple models and benchmarks

long_term_memory: LangMem: Modular framework supporting multiple memory types
A-Mem: Zettelkasten-inspired design linking knowledge units
Mem0: Scalable extract-update pipeline with graph variant
Zep: Temporal knowledge graph for cross-session reasoning
short_term_memory: ReSum: Periodic compression of interaction histories
reinforcement_learning: Limitations: Existing RL systems treat memory as static/external component

limitations: Fixed set of memory management tools - could be extended for more fine-grained control
Limited to evaluated benchmarks - broader task coverage may strengthen empirical understanding
conclusion: AgeMem replaces heuristic memory pipelines with an end-to-end optimized solution, improving both task performance and memory quality while maintaining efficient context usage. Highlights the importance of unified, agent-centric memory policies for building scalable and adaptive LLM agents capable of long-term reasoning.
